entities.name: GLP-1/amylin receptor co-agonist polypeptide
entities.context: The invention relates to compounds functioning as dual receptor agonists that bind to and activate both GLP-1 and amylin receptors. These co-agonists are single polypeptide molecules rather than separate co-administered formulations, designed for treating obesity, type 2 diabetes mellitus, cardiovascular disease, and related metabolic conditions.

entities.context: CagriSema is a co-administered combination of cagrilintide (a long-acting amylin analog) and semaglutide (a GLP-1 receptor agonist) currently in Phase 3 clinical trials.

This patent relates to the CagriSema program. CagriSema is a co-administered combination of cagrilintide (a long-acting amylin analog) and semaglutide (a GLP-1 receptor agonist) currently in Phase 3 clinical trials.
Patent ID: US12054528B2, Assignee: Novo Nordisk AS, Filing Date: December 19, 2022
CagriSema is a co-administered combination of cagrilintide (a long-acting amylin analog) and semaglutide (a GLP-1 receptor agonist) currently in Phase 3 clinical trials.
CagriSema is a co-administered combination of cagrilintide (a long-acting amylin analog) and semaglutide (a GLP-1 receptor agonist) currently in Phase 3 clinical trials.
Phase 2 data showed the combination achieved superior weight loss versus either monotherapy without worsening tolerability.
The combination targets both GLP-1-mediated glucose-dependent insulin secretion and appetite suppression, and amylin-mediated gastric emptying delay and central satiety signaling.

CagriSema is a co-administered combination of cagrilintide (a long-acting amylin analog) and semaglutide (a GLP-1 receptor agonist) currently in Phase 3 clinical trials.
This patent represents a next-generation approach: engineering a single molecule with dual GLP-1/amylin activity rather than co-administering two separate peptides.
